data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 moved from oper up to lower-layer-down while admin is up; R3 Serial0/1/1 moved from oper up to down and admin set to down. Impact: point-to-point serial link(s) between routers are down causing loss of connectivity. Desired outcome: restore Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 to oper up with correct admin state so connectivity is resumed.

data.intent_description: Serial0/1/1 on R2 moved from oper up to lower-layer-down while admin is up; Serial0/1/1 on R3 moved from oper up to down and admin set to down. Impact: point-to-point serial link between R2 and R3 is down causing loss of connectivity. Desired outcome: restore Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 to oper up with correct admin state so connectivity is resumed.

plan.problem: Point-to-point link Serial0/1/1 between router2 and router3 is down: router2 oper=lower-layer-down admin=up; router3 oper=down admin=down. Connectivity lost.
plan.fix_summary: Bring interface admin state to up on both endpoints to reinitialize the serial link: (1) issue no shutdown on router2 Serial0/1/1 (restarts line protocol negotiation) and (2) issue no shutdown on router3 Serial0/1/1 (undo admin down). Operational risk: temporary traffic interruption and possible interface flap during re-establishment.

messages.content:
What was observed
- The point-to-point link Serial0/1/1 between R2 and R3 was down: R2 showed admin=up / oper=lower-layer-down; R3 showed admin=down / oper=down. Connectivity between the routers was lost.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R3's interface was administratively down (and the link negotiation was not established), causing the serial circuit to be down.

Changes made
- Issued no shutdown on Serial0/1/1 for both endpoints (attempts to set interface state to up). The devices reported the interfaces were enabled/already up.

Verification
- Passed. Current observations: R2 Serial0/1/1 admin=up oper=up (no input/output errors); R3 Serial0/1/1 admin=up oper=up (no input/output errors); OSPF on R2 shows Serial0/1/1 in Point-to-Point with neighbor 3.3.3.3 in FULL. 
- Remaining issues: none. Connectivity restored.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- The point-to-point link Serial0/1/1 between R2 and R3 was down: R2 showed admin=up / oper=lower-layer-down; R3 showed admin=down / oper=down. Connectivity between the routers was lost.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R3's interface was administratively down (and the link negotiation was not established), causing the serial circuit to be down.

Changes made
- Issued no shutdown on Serial0/1/1 for both endpoints (attempts to set interface state to up). The devices reported the interfaces were enabled/already up.

Verification
- Passed. Current observations: R2 Serial0/1/1 admin=up oper=up (no input/output errors); R3 Serial0/1/1 admin=up oper=up (no input/output errors); OSPF on R2 shows Serial0/1/1 in Point-to-Point with neighbor 3.3.3.3 in FULL. 
- Remaining issues: none. Connectivity restored.
